questions.prompt: Are you asking about your own record?
options: I am helping someone complete their own check

questions.prompt: Did this case happen in Iowa (not a federal case)?

options: Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar program
Juvenile adjudication or offense committed as a minor

Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or.label: The case was dropped or thrown out
Dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or.helperText: dismissed, no-billed, nolle prosequi, or not prosecuted

Diversion, deferred disposition, supervi.label: Completed a program instead of a conviction
Diversion, deferred disposition, supervi.helperText: diversion, deferred disposition, supervision, or similar

Juvenile adjudication or offense committ.label: It happened when I was a minor
Juvenile adjudication or offense committ.helperText: juvenile adjudication or offense as a minor

I am not sure.label: I'm not sure
I am not sure.helperText: That's okay — we'll help you figure it out

questions.prompt: What kind of charge was it?
Do any of these sound like your situation?
options: Acquittal or all-charges-dismissed expungement under Iowa Code § 901C.2
Misdemeanor conviction expungement under Iowa Code § 901C.3
Public-intoxication conviction expungement under Iowa Code § 123.46

None of these / I am not sure
questions.helper: This answer is optional and does not select your legal pathway. The backend uses the factual answers and state rules.

questions.prompt: How old were you when this happened?
Have you paid off everything the court charged?
Have you had a record cleared before, anywhere?
Which county (or local area) handled the case?
Which court or agency handled the case?
What does the record say you were charged with?
questions.helperText: the charge name or code, however it's written

questions.prompt: Do you have your court paperwork handy?
questions.helperText: No worries if not — you can save and come back.
also called a docket, cause, or arrest number — it's on your court paperwork

pathways.label: Acquittal or all-charges-dismissed expungement under Iowa Code § 901C.2
pathways.summary: Every criminal charge in the case must have resulted in acquittal or dismissal. All court costs, fees, and other financial obligations for the case must be satisfied. More than 180 days must have passed after acquittal or dismissal unless the court waives that requirement. The case cannot be a not-guilty-by-reason-of-insanity disposition, and the defendant cannot have been found incompetent to stand trial. The source says § 901C.2 does not apply to a dismissal related to deferred judgment under…
ruleClauses: Every criminal charge in the case must have resulted in acquittal or dismissal.
All court costs, fees, and other financial obligations for the case must be satisfied.
More than 180 days must have passed after acquittal or dismissal unless the court waives that requirement.
The case cannot be a not-guilty-by-reason-of-insanity disposition, and the defendant cannot have been found incompetent to stand trial.
The source says § 901C.2 does not apply to a dismissal related to deferred judgment under § 907.9.
A copy of the application must be provided to the county attorney.
waitingRules: More than 180 days must have passed after acquittal or dismissal unless the court waives that requirement.
exclusionRules: The case cannot be a not-guilty-by-reason-of-insanity disposition, and the defendant cannot have been found incompetent to stand trial.
The source says § 901C.2 does not apply to a dismissal related to deferred judgment under § 907.9.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Misdemeanor conviction expungement under Iowa Code § 901C.3
pathways.summary: Only one § 901C.3 misdemeanor expungement is allowed in a lifetime. Multiple cases must arise from the same transaction or occurrence and the application must be filed separately in each case. The offense must not be on the ineligible statutory list stated in the form. All court costs, fees, fines, restitution, and other financial obligations must be paid in full. More than eight years must have passed since the misdemeanor conviction. No criminal charge may be pending, and the person cannot…
ruleClauses: Only one § 901C.3 misdemeanor expungement is allowed in a lifetime.
Multiple cases must arise from the same transaction or occurrence and the application must be filed separately in each case.
The offense must not be on the ineligible statutory list stated in the form.
All court costs, fees, fines, restitution, and other financial obligations must be paid in full.
More than eight years must have passed since the misdemeanor conviction.
No criminal charge may be pending, and the person cannot have previously received more than one deferred judgment.
An official Iowa DCI criminal-history check dated within 30 days of filing must be attached.
waitingRules: More than eight years must have passed since the misdemeanor conviction.
An official Iowa DCI criminal-history check dated within 30 days of filing must be attached.
exclusionRules: No criminal charge may be pending, and the person cannot have previously received more than one deferred judgment.

frontendBranch.entryCondition: Backend matched this jurisdiction-specific route after evaluating the cited source rules.

pathways.label: Public-intoxication conviction expungement under Iowa Code § 123.46
pathways.summary: The source requires a conviction under § 123.46 or a similar local ordinance. The person must have no criminal conviction other than specified traffic/simple-misdemeanor exceptions during the two-year period after conviction. The application must be served on the county attorney.
ruleClauses: The source requires a conviction under § 123.46 or a similar local ordinance.
The person must have no criminal conviction other than specified traffic/simple-misdemeanor exceptions during the two-year period after conviction.
The application must be served on the county attorney.
waitingRules: The person must have no criminal conviction other than specified traffic/simple-misdemeanor exceptions during the two-year period after conviction.

ruleClauses: The source requires a conviction under § 123.47 or a similar local ordinance.

waitingRules: The person must have no criminal conviction other than specified traffic/simple-misdemeanor exceptions during the two-year period after conviction.

pathways.label: Prostitution conviction expungement for conduct while under 18 under Iowa Code § 725.1
pathways.summary: The person must have been under age 18 when the acts underlying the prostitution conviction occurred. The person must have no criminal conviction other than specified traffic/simple-misdemeanor exceptions during the two-year period after conviction. The application must be served on the county attorney.
ruleClauses: The person must have been under age 18 when the acts underlying the prostitution conviction occurred.
The person must have no criminal conviction other than specified traffic/simple-misdemeanor exceptions during the two-year period after conviction.
The application must be served on the county attorney.
waitingRules: The person must have no criminal conviction other than specified traffic/simple-misdemeanor exceptions during the two-year period after conviction.

generatorSelectionContract.failureBehavior: Return needs_more_info or needs_review. Never fall back to a generic packet or another jurisdiction generator.

packet_ready.title: You may have a packet-ready path.

packet_ready_with_caution.title: You may have a path, with cautions.

needs_more_info.title: We need a few more details.

guidance_only.title: We can give you state-specific next steps.

not_covered_yet.title: This record type is not supported by the current packet set.

likely_not_eligible.title: This record may not match a self-help filing path.

needs_review.title: This situation needs review before a packet is generated.

hard_stop.title: This tool cannot prepare a packet for this matter.

copyGuardrails: Use Iowa-specific remedy terminology returned by the profile; do not relabel every remedy as expungement.
Use “may qualify,” “possible path,” and “the court or agency makes the final decision.”
Do not promise erasure, sealing, eligibility, timing, filing acceptance, or a court outcome.
Do not show checkout unless the backend returns a packet-ready result and paymentAllowed=true.

sourceSections.title:  The misdemeanor criminal convictions covered by this application are eligible for
sourceSections.text:
expungement. NOTE: Convictions under Iowa Code sections 123.46, 123.47(3) (or similar local
ordinance), 235B.20, 321.218, 321A.32, 321J.21, 321J.2, 707.5, 708.2(3), 708.2A, 708.7, 708.11, 708.12,
716.8(3) or (4), 721.2, 721.10, 723.1; or chapters 717C, 719, 720, 721.2, 724, 726, 728, 901A; or a sex
offense as defined in section 692A.101 are ineligible.

5.  All court costs, fees, fines, restitution, and any other financial obligations ordered
by the court or assessed by the clerk of district court in relation to the
misdemeanor convictions covered by this application have been paid in full.

sourceSections.title:  I have had no criminal convictions other than local traffic violations or simple
sourceSections.text:
misdemeanor violations under chapter 321 during the two-year period following
the conviction.
Read Before Signing
Please check each statement below after you have read it.
 I understand that I must provide a copy of this application to the county attorney.
 I understand that the records in a criminal case expunged under this section are
confidential and exempt from public access under Iowa Code section 22.7. The
record shall not be accessible except by court order.

Continue to next page

vs. Application to Expunge Possession of
Alcohol under the Legal Age
Court Records under
Defendant Iowa Code section 123.47

If you need assistance to participate in court due to a disability,
call the disability coordinator (information at
www.iowacourts.gov/for-the-public/ada). Persons who are
hearing or speech impaired may call Relay Iowa TTY (1-800-
735-2942). Disability coordinators cannot provide legal
advice.

The undersigned Defendant respectfully applies to the court for an order expunging the
court records in the above-captioned case pursuant to Iowa Code section 123.47(9). In
support of this application, Defendant states that the following statements are true and
correct to the best of Defendant’s knowledge:
Read, complete, and check each item if you agree.
1.  I was convicted for a violation of Iowa Code section 123.47, possession of
alcohol under the legal age, or of a similar local ordinance on:
,
